domain: Test-Time Compute Scaling and Inference-Time Reasoning for SLMs

scoring_methodology.demand: 0-10 scale based on validated signals (forum requests, GitHub issues, research papers, industry adoption)
scoring_methodology.competition: 0-10 scale where 10 = nobody does this, 0 = saturated market
scoring_methodology.feasibility: 0-10 scale based on technical complexity, available building blocks, and time-to-MVP

ranked_gaps.title: Plug-and-Play Reasoning Enhancement Layer for Existing SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A model-agnostic Python SDK that wraps ANY SLM (Phi, Qwen, Gemma, Llama-small) with reasoning search strategies (MCTS, beam search, self-consistency, best-of-N) via a simple API: `reasoner = ReasonEnhance(model); result = reasoner.solve(prompt, strategy='mcts', budget=10)`. Includes pre-built PRM adapters, automatic strategy selection based on query type, and OpenAI-compatible API endpoints. Integrates with vLLM/SGLang backends for serving.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Application developers (millions globally) who use SLMs but lack ML expertise to implement search algorithms. Startups building AI products on open-source SLMs. Enterprise teams wanting better reasoning without switching models. Estimated 500K+ developers deploying SLMs in production by mid-2026.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLM deployment is exploding (Phi-4-mini, Qwen3, Gemma 3 all released in 2025-2026) but reasoning enhancement requires stitching 3+ libraries together. HuggingFace search-and-learn (1.1k stars) proves demand but requires model-specific integration. Solution Guidance Fine-Tuning (SGFT) research shows plug-and-play reasoning is technically feasible. The gap between SLM deployment ease and reasoning enhancement complexity is widening.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $200M-500M TAM (subset of LLM tooling/inference market, comparable to LangChain/LlamaIndex ecosystem)

ranked_gaps.title: Adaptive Compute Budget Controller for SLM Reasoning
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A middleware service that dynamically allocates test-time compute based on query difficulty and latency SLOs. Features: difficulty classifier trained on SLM confidence signals, configurable compute budget tiers (1x-100x), real-time latency SLO enforcement, cost tracking dashboard, and integration with vLLM/SGLang serving stacks. Implements budget forcing (s1-style), Dynasor-style dynamic allocation, and conformal risk control for compute budgets.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Production ML teams serving reasoning SLMs at scale (estimated 50K+ teams globally). Cloud AI platform teams (AWS, Azure, GCP). Cost-conscious startups where reasoning model inference is their largest expense. Any team paying for GPU compute on reasoning workloads.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Dynasor proved 50% compute savings + 3.3x throughput is achievable but remains research-only. s1 budget forcing was ICLR 2025 Oral showing academic validation. NVIDIA NIM now has thinking budget control, validating vendor interest. Anthropic and OpenAI both ship thinking budget parameters. An entire survey paper exists on this exact gap ('Reasoning on a Budget', Jul 2025). Conformal Thinking (Feb 2026) adds statistical guarantees. No open-source production tool exists yet.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $300M-700M TAM (subset of inference optimization market, adjacent to GPU cost optimization tools like RunPod, Modal)

ranked_gaps.title: Unified SLM Reasoning SDK: Search + PRM + Serving in One Package
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An integrated platform combining: (1) reasoning search algorithms (MCTS, beam search, ToT, self-consistency, best-of-N), (2) PRM training and inference (with SLM-optimized compact verifiers), and (3) production serving with batched reasoning (vLLM/SGLang integration). Single config file defines the full reasoning pipeline. Includes a CLI for experimentation and a server mode for production. Ships with pre-trained PRMs for popular SLMs.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers at startups and mid-size companies (500K+ globally) building reasoning-enhanced SLM applications. Research labs wanting reproducible reasoning experiments. Platform teams building internal AI infrastructure. Companies transitioning from API-based reasoning (o1/o3) to self-hosted SLM reasoning.
ranked_gaps.why_now: The landscape is deeply fragmented: OpenR has search+PRM but no serving, vLLM/SGLang have serving but no reasoning search, HuggingFace search-and-learn covers only search. PaCoRe achieves GPT-5-level results with 8B models but is research-only. ScalR 2025 workshop (first on test-time scaling) signals field maturation. Multiple awesome-lists (5+ repos, 500+ stars each) document the fragmentation. TTA* (A*-search for SLMs) shows training-free approaches are production-viable.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $250M-600M TAM (reasoning infrastructure market, growing as SLM deployment scales)

ranked_gaps.title: Reasoning Distillation Pipeline: Large Reasoner -> Small Reasoner
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An end-to-end pipeline tool: (1) generate reasoning traces from teacher models (o3, R1, QwQ) via API or local inference, (2) automated trace quality filtering using PRM scoring and rule-based heuristics, (3) train student SLM with SFT + optional RL using TRL/Unsloth backends, (4) evaluate with reasoning-specific metrics (step accuracy, chain quality, compute efficiency), (5) iterate with active learning. CLI-driven with YAML configs. Supports progressive distillation (70B -> 7B -> 1B).
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams building custom small reasoning models (estimated 10K+ teams globally). Enterprises wanting to reduce API costs by distilling frontier reasoning into owned SLMs. Research labs studying reasoning transfer. Mobile/edge AI teams needing compact reasoners.
ranked_gaps.why_now: DeepSeek-R1 distillation (Jan 2025) proved reasoning transfers to 1.5B models. Microsoft Phi-4 trained via o3-mini distillation validates approach. HuggingFace showed Llama 3B outperforming 70B with test-time compute. Modular distillation framework (ICLR 2026) breaks task into Analyzer/Informant/Reasoner components. But no integrated tool exists — practitioners must stitch together 4-5 libraries manually. Red Hat documented the pain in 'Lessons reproducing R1-like reasoning'.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $200M-500M TAM (subset of model training/fine-tuning market, growing as distillation becomes standard practice)

ranked_gaps.title: On-Device / Edge Reasoning Search for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A lightweight reasoning search library optimized for mobile/edge deployment: (1) memory-efficient MCTS/beam search that fits in <500MB RAM overhead, (2) latency-aware search with 10-100ms budgets, (3) quantized PRM inference co-located with SLM, (4) CoreML/TFLite/ONNX export for search components, (5) progressive search that returns best-so-far answer at any interrupt point. Targets Phi-4-mini, Gemma 3 270M-1B, and DeepSeek-R1-1.5B on iPhone/Android/Jetson.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Mobile AI developers (millions globally), edge computing teams at enterprises, IoT companies, Apple/Android ecosystem developers, robotics teams, and defense/aerospace applications requiring on-device reasoning without cloud connectivity.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Phi-4-mini runs at 12+ tokens/sec on iPhone 14 but with greedy decoding only. Dell predicts 2026 as 'the year of edge AI'. Liquid AI ships LFM2.5-1.2B for on-device but without search. Step-level Beam Search (SBS) is more efficient than full MCTS — suitable for edge. Edge AI market projected to reach $20B+ by 2030. No framework specifically optimizes reasoning search for mobile latency/memory constraints.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $300M-800M TAM (subset of edge AI market, growing rapidly as on-device LLM deployment becomes mainstream)

ranked_gaps.title: Hybrid Edge-Cloud Reasoning Orchestration
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An orchestration framework that intelligently splits reasoning between edge SLM and cloud LLM: (1) on-device difficulty classifier decides what to handle locally vs. offload, (2) edge SLM handles easy reasoning steps with local MCTS, (3) only offloads hard sub-problems to cloud LLM, (4) graceful degradation when offline, (5) privacy-aware routing (sensitive data stays on device). Implements ECO-LLM and HybridFlow patterns in a production-ready SDK.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams building AI for intermittent connectivity (healthcare field ops, military, rural areas). Privacy-sensitive domains (legal, medical, financial). Cost-conscious mobile AI teams wanting cloud-quality reasoning at edge-level cost. Estimated 100K+ enterprise teams with edge-cloud AI needs.
ranked_gaps.why_now: ECO-LLM, HybridFlow, CE-CoLLM, and Walle all emerged in 2025-2026 as research frameworks validating the approach. ACM survey (Jul 2025) comprehensively documents edge-cloud SLM collaboration. MoE-based routing for edge-cloud is proven. But no production SDK exists — all are research prototypes. Enterprise demand is clear from healthcare, defense, and industrial IoT sectors.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $200M-600M TAM (subset of edge-cloud AI orchestration market)

ranked_gaps.title: Domain-Specific Reasoning Enhancement for SLMs (Beyond Math)
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: Domain-specific reasoning enhancement kits for SLMs covering legal, medical, and financial reasoning: (1) domain-specific PRMs (building on Fin-PRM and VersaPRM patterns), (2) domain-aware search heuristics that encode domain rules, (3) evaluation benchmarks per domain, (4) curated training data for domain PRM training. Ship as plug-in modules for the reasoning SDK (GAP-010). Start with financial reasoning (Fin-PRM shows 12.9% gains) then expand.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Vertical AI companies in legal tech ($30B market), healthcare AI ($45B market), and fintech ($25B market). Regulated industries needing domain-specific reasoning with compliance guardrails. Enterprise AI teams building vertical products.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Fin-PRM (2025) proved domain-specialized PRMs yield 12.9% improvement in financial reasoning. VersaPRM shows multi-domain PRMs achieve 7.9% gains in legal reasoning. Nearly all existing test-time compute tools focus on math/code benchmarks — massive blind spot. 60%+ of financial institutions are running AI pilots in 2026. Domain specialization identified as key to making LLMs disruptive (ACM Computing Surveys 2026).
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $400M-1B TAM (vertical AI reasoning tools across healthcare, legal, financial sectors)

ranked_gaps.title: Reasoning Chain Compression / Efficient CoT for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A library for compressing reasoning chains to reduce serving cost while preserving accuracy: (1) TokenSkip-style selective token skipping (40% reduction, <0.4% accuracy drop), (2) Compressed Chain-of-Thought (CCoT) with dense continuous representations, (3) difficulty-aware distillation that generates shorter chains for easy problems, (4) configurable compression ratio with accuracy guarantees. Ships as middleware between reasoning search and final output.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Cost-sensitive production teams serving reasoning SLMs (every token costs proportionally more for small models). High-throughput API providers. Mobile/edge teams where token generation directly impacts latency and battery life. Estimated 100K+ teams running reasoning workloads where CoT tokens are 10-100x the output.
ranked_gaps.why_now: TokenSkip (EMNLP 2025) achieves 40% token reduction with only 0.2% LoRA fine-tuning. CCoT uses continuous embeddings for compressed reasoning. ImgCoT renders CoT as images for spatial reasoning compression. Chain of Draft ('Thinking Faster by Writing Less') gained viral attention. 'When More is Less' paper documents over-reasoning as a real problem. Energy costs of test-time compute are now a public concern. No production tool combines these research advances.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $150M-400M TAM (inference cost optimization, adjacent to model compression tools market)

ranked_gaps.title: Cost-Optimal Reasoning Strategy Selector
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A tool that automatically recommends the cheapest reasoning approach for a target accuracy: (1) benchmarks user's SLM with multiple strategies (greedy, CoT, self-consistency, MCTS, best-of-N) on representative queries, (2) builds cost-accuracy Pareto curves, (3) recommends optimal strategy per query type, (4) integrates with routing systems to dynamically select strategy at inference time. Includes cost calculator comparing SLM+search vs. larger model+greedy vs. API-based reasoning.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Engineering managers making build-vs-buy decisions on reasoning infrastructure. Cost-conscious ML teams (60%+ of AI budgets go to inference). Platform teams evaluating SLM+reasoning vs. API-based reasoning models. Finance/ops teams needing cost projections for reasoning workloads.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Cost-aware contrastive routing (CSCR) improves accuracy-cost tradeoff by 25%. Cascade routing framework proves optimal strategy selection is tractable. Real-world routing achieves 60% cost savings vs. uniform GPT-4o. Jensen Huang highlighted 100x compute increase for reasoning models. No tool helps practitioners choose between SLM+MCTS vs. larger model+greedy — a decision every production team faces.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $100M-300M TAM (AI cost optimization tools, adjacent to FinOps/CloudOps market)

ranked_gaps.title: Reasoning Quality Observability & Monitoring
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An observability platform for reasoning models in production: (1) step-level correctness tracking using lightweight PRM scoring, (2) reasoning chain length and token usage analytics, (3) compute-vs-accuracy dashboards per query type, (4) anomaly detection when reasoning quality degrades, (5) A/B testing for reasoning strategies, (6) integration with existing observability stacks (Datadog, Grafana, Arize). Ships as an OpenTelemetry-compatible plugin.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: MLOps teams operating reasoning models in production (20K+ teams globally). Platform engineering teams building AI infrastructure. SRE teams responsible for AI service reliability. Product teams wanting to understand reasoning quality trends.
ranked_gaps.why_now: LLM observability became a mandatory infrastructure layer in 2026. OpenAI published 'Monitoring Monitorability' acknowledging CoT monitoring as critical for safety. 20+ observability platforms exist but NONE have reasoning-specific metrics (step accuracy, chain quality, reasoning anomalies). Arize, LangSmith, Braintrust offer generic LLM tracing but cannot evaluate reasoning step quality. Chain of thought monitorability identified as 'fragile opportunity' for AI safety.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $150M-400M TAM (LLM observability market projected at $2B+ by 2027, reasoning-specific is a growing subset)

ranked_gaps.title: Multi-SLM Reasoning Ensemble with Routing
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A production-ready system that routes queries across multiple SLMs and reasoning strategies: (1) query classifier that predicts optimal model+strategy combination, (2) ensemble voting across heterogeneous SLMs (Phi + Qwen + Gemma), (3) cascading fallback from fast/cheap to slow/accurate, (4) latency and cost budget enforcement, (5) online learning to improve routing decisions over time. Implements SLM-MUX and Aladdin patterns in production.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Production teams wanting best accuracy at lowest cost across diverse query types. API providers serving heterogeneous workloads. Enterprise AI platforms supporting multiple use cases with a single system.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SLM-MUX showed 13.4% improvement via SLM ensembling. Aladdin achieves 71% GPU cost savings with routing. CSCR improves accuracy-cost tradeoff by 25%. Multiple cloud providers offer model routing but NOT reasoning-strategy routing. The explosion of high-quality SLMs (Phi-4, Qwen3, Gemma 3) makes heterogeneous ensembles viable for the first time.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $150M-400M TAM (AI inference routing market, growing with multi-model deployment)

ranked_gaps.title: SLM-Specific Process Reward Model Training Toolkit
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A specialized toolkit for training compact PRMs that run alongside SLMs: (1) efficient step-level data generation using Monte Carlo rollouts (AgentPRM-style), (2) PRM quantization-aware training for edge deployment, (3) shared-backbone PRM that reuses SLM weights (minimal additional memory), (4) InversePRM for learning from failures, (5) domain-specific PRM templates. Integrates with HuggingFace TRL and axolotl for training backends.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Researchers and engineers building verifiers for small reasoning models. Teams deploying reasoning SLMs where PRM memory budget is critical. Anyone using test-time compute scaling (PRM is the bottleneck for quality).
ranked_gaps.why_now: sdiehl/prm library exists but not SLM-optimized. AgentPRM shows 3B models can outperform GPT-4o with proper PRM training. ThinkPRM needs only 1% of PRM800K labels. InversePRM enables learning from failures. Fin-PRM demonstrates domain-specialized PRMs. But no toolkit combines these advances into an SLM-friendly package. PRM training remains the hardest part of test-time compute scaling.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $100M-250M TAM (subset of ML model training tools market)

ranked_gaps.title: Streaming Reasoning Chains with Progressive Refinement
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A middleware layer that streams intermediate reasoning steps to users with progressive answer refinement: (1) parse reasoning tree into displayable steps as they are generated, (2) show confidence score per step, (3) allow users to stop when answer is 'good enough', (4) progressive answer refinement that improves over time, (5) interactive controls (pause, redirect, branch). Ships as React/SDK component and API middleware compatible with any reasoning backend.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Product teams building user-facing reasoning applications (chatbots, coding assistants, search). UX designers implementing AI interfaces. Enterprise teams where users need transparency into AI reasoning. Estimated 200K+ product teams building AI UIs.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Think-as-You-See (TaYS) framework demonstrates streaming CoT for vision-language models. StreamingThinker shows models can 'think while reading'. ShapeofAI documents 'Stream of Thought' as an emerging UX pattern. Interactive reasoning tree visualization exists in research. ai-sdk provides Chain of Thought component. But no production middleware combines streaming reasoning with progressive refinement and user controls.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $100M-300M TAM (subset of AI UX tooling market)

ranked_gaps.title: Self-Improving SLM Reasoning Loop (Online Learning)
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A production system enabling SLMs to continuously improve reasoning from deployment feedback: (1) online collection of reasoning successes/failures with user signals, (2) periodic PRM update loop using new data, (3) ReasoningBank-style memory for retrieving similar past reasoning, (4) automatic search strategy refinement based on performance metrics, (5) safety guardrails to prevent regression. Implements Google's ReasoningBank (20% improvement on 1.7B model) in production.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams wanting reasoning models that improve over time without manual retraining. Enterprise AI teams with domain-specific feedback loops. Production systems where user corrections are available.
ranked_gaps.why_now: ICLR 2026 Workshop on Recursive Self-Improvement shows community maturation. ReasoningBank (Sep 2025) proved 20% improvement on 1.7B models via memory-based retrieval. Self-Improving LLM Agents paper (Oct 2025) provides test-time self-improvement methods. STILL-2 validates offline self-improvement loop. But online/production self-improvement remains research-only with significant safety concerns.

ranked_gaps.title: Reasoning-Aware Quantization and Pruning
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A model compression toolkit that specifically preserves reasoning capability: (1) reasoning-aware quantization that tests step-level accuracy (not just perplexity), (2) pruning guided by reasoning-critical attention heads/layers, (3) quantization-aware PRM co-training, (4) automated benchmark suite testing reasoning before/after compression, (5) export to mobile formats with reasoning quality guarantees.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams deploying reasoning SLMs on constrained hardware (mobile, edge, embedded). Model compression teams at cloud providers. Anyone quantizing reasoning models who needs quality guarantees.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Phi-4-mini runs on mobile with 4-bit quantization but reasoning quality impact is unknown. Standard quantization methods degrade reasoning disproportionately (documented in multiple papers). Edge AI market demands compressed models. But no tool specifically evaluates or preserves reasoning capability during compression.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $80M-200M TAM (subset of model compression tools market)

ranked_gaps.title: Open-Source Reasoning Benchmark Suite for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A standardized benchmark suite for comparing SLM reasoning approaches under realistic constraints: (1) compute-efficiency curves (accuracy vs. FLOPs), (2) latency-accuracy tradeoff metrics, (3) memory-bounded performance tests, (4) on-device feasibility scores, (5) domain-specific reasoning benchmarks beyond math/code. Includes leaderboard and reproducible evaluation harness.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Researchers comparing SLM reasoning methods (thousands globally). Companies evaluating which SLM + reasoning strategy to deploy. Conference reviewers needing standardized baselines.
ranked_gaps.why_now: testtimescaling.github.io survey proposes evaluation dimensions but no standardized implementation. Papers compare on different subsets making fair comparison impossible. The field needs a shared evaluation framework as it matures from research to production.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $20M-50M TAM (benchmark/evaluation tools are typically open-source with limited direct revenue, but high strategic value)

ranked_gaps.title: Multi-Modal Reasoning at Test-Time for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A framework applying MCTS/beam search/self-consistency to multi-modal SLM reasoning (vision + language): (1) multi-modal search trees that branch on both text and visual reasoning, (2) multi-modal PRMs that verify visual reasoning steps, (3) integration with vision-language SLMs (Phi-4-vision, Gemma 3). Extends TaYS streaming CoT pattern to general multi-modal reasoning.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams building multi-modal AI applications (document understanding, visual QA, robotics). Research labs exploring multi-modal reasoning.
ranked_gaps.why_now: TaYS (Mar 2026) demonstrates streaming CoT for vision-language models. Phi-4-reasoning-vision exists but without search enhancement. Multi-modal SLMs are proliferating. But multi-modal reasoning search is essentially unexplored — high novelty, high risk.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $100M-300M TAM (multi-modal AI tooling, still early stage)

ranked_gaps.title: Interactive Reasoning Debugger for SLMs
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A visual debugging tool for inspecting, replaying, and editing reasoning search trees: (1) interactive MCTS tree visualization with node-level scores, (2) step-through replay of beam search candidates, (3) manual node editing to test counterfactual reasoning paths, (4) PRM score overlay on reasoning tree, (5) comparison view for different strategies on same query. Web-based UI with Python SDK for programmatic access.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML researchers and engineers developing reasoning systems (estimated 10K+ globally). Teams debugging reasoning failures in production. Anyone tuning search hyperparameters for SLM reasoning.
ranked_gaps.why_now: LLM Reasoners has basic visualization but not interactive debugging. Interactive reasoning tree visualization exists in research (Jun 2025 paper). As reasoning systems move to production, debugging becomes critical. But this is a developer tool with limited market size.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: $30M-80M TAM (ML developer tools, comparable to TensorBoard/Weights & Biases niche features)
